So the doc `dual-emitting-using-warp.md` saying to use `.cts` extension for CJS polyfills is correct per the warp documentation and current code, even though the memory says otherwise. Warp watch specifically looks for `.ts`/`.mts`/`.cts` changes.

These are two separate approaches, so the doc about `.cts` for polyfill substitution is still valid. The memory's note about renaming applies to the import resolution approach, not the polyfill approach.

2026-04-13T09:43:37.8424215Z ◆ The actual perf test template uses `tsconfig.lib.json`, not `tsconfig.package`. The doc `writing-performance-tests.md` shows:
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8425108Z   ```json
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8425275Z   {
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8425465Z     "extends": "../../../../tsconfig.package",
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8425718Z   ```
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8425865Z   
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8426439Z   This is wrong. The correct file to extend would be `../../../../tsconfig.lib.json` for a project 4 levels deep (perf-test of a service at `sdk/<service>/perf-tests/<package>`).
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8427079Z   
2026-04-13T09:43:37.8428100Z   Wait, the path `../../../../tsconfig.package` suggests the file is at 4 directories deep. The perf test template is at `sdk/template/template-perf-tests`, which is 3 levels deep (`sdk/template/template-perf-tests` → extends `../../../tsconfig.lib.json`).

2026-04-13T09:43:37.8455788Z

The actual implementation in `sdk/identity/identity/src/credentials/defaultAzureCredential.ts` tries **eight** credentials in order. Added the missing six:\n- `WorkloadIdentityCredential`\n- `VisualStudioCodeCredential`\n- `AzureCliCredential`\n- `AzurePowerShellCredential`\n- `AzureDeveloperCliCredential`\n- `BrokerCredential` (with a note that it requires `@azure/identity-broker`)\n\n### `writing-performance-tests.md` — non-existent `tsconfig.package` reference\nThe example `tsconfig.json` for a perf test project used `\"extends\": \"../../../../tsconfig.package\"`. No `tsconfig.package.json` file exists at the repo root. The actual template perf test (`sdk/template/template-perf-tests/tsconfig.json`) extends `tsconfig.lib.json`, so the example was updated to `\"extends\": \"../../../../tsconfig.lib.json\"`.\n\n### `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — missing `/` in `git add` one-liner\nThe combined shell command that resolves the merge conflict in one step had a typo: the `git add` part was missing the `/` path separator between the shell-substituted repo root and `pnpm-lock.yaml`:\n```shell\n# Before (broken)\ngit add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n# After (fixed)\ngit add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n```\n\n### `steps-after-generations.md` — missing `...` suffix on three `pnpm build --filter` commands\nThree code blocks (Windows record, Linux playback, Windows playback) used `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` without the trailing `...`. Per repo conventions (and `CONTRIBUTING.md`), the `...` suffix is required to also build dependencies. The Linux record block already had `...`; the other three were inconsistent and have been fixed.\n","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","integrity":"high","secrecy":"public","title":"docs: fix inconsistencies in documentation folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}
